sql_array: CREATE TABLE test_table (id INT, value FLOAT, category VARCHAR(10));
INSERT INTO test_table VALUES (1, 10.5, 'A'), (2, NULL, 'B'), (3, 5.0, 'A'), (4, 20.0, 'B'), (5, 15.5, NULL);
SELECT arg_max_null(value, id) AS max_id FROM test_table WHERE category = 'A' GROUP BY category HAVING COUNT(*) > 1;
SELECT (SELECT value FROM test_table WHERE id = (SELECT arg_max_null(id, value) FROM test_table WHERE category = 'B')) - 2 AS adjusted_value;
CREATE TABLE another_table (ref_id INT, ref_value VARCHAR(10));

SELECT a.ref_id, a.ref_value, arg_max_null(a.ref_value, CAST(a.ref_value AS INT) - 5) AS max_adjusted_value FROM another_table AS a JOIN test_table AS t ON a.ref_id = t.id GROUP BY a.ref_id, a.ref_value HAVING max_adjusted_value IS NOT NULL;
SELECT * FROM (SELECT category, arg_max_null(value, (value - 10)) AS max_value_adjusted FROM test_table GROUP BY category) subquery WHERE max_value_adjusted IS NOT NULL;
SELECT (SELECT arg_max_null(value, id) FROM test_table WHERE value IS NOT NULL) - (SELECT COUNT(*) FROM ever_empty_table) AS final_result;
